params.label: Pyramid scale
params.tip: Number of resolution levels in the image pyramid. Higher values allow smoother zooming for very large images. 2 is suitable for most images.

params.label: Convert to 8-bit
params.tip: Rescale 16-bit intensities to 8-bit on import (halves storage; mirrors the Fiji rescale-then-save-8-bit workflow). The 16-bit is transient and deleted — only the 8-bit is kept. Use for large low-dynamic-range acquisitions.

params.label: Copy to local scratch first
params.tip: Copy the source (and its companion files, e.g. Olympus OIR _000nn parts) to local disk before converting. Reading a multi-file format directly over a network share (SMB) is very slow — a bulk local copy first is far faster. The copy is deleted after import.

params.label: 8-bit low percentile
params.tip: Bottom of the per-channel intensity window when converting to 8-bit. 0 = the channel's true minimum. Raise it to clip a background floor.

params.label: 8-bit high percentile
params.tip: Top of the per-channel intensity window when converting to 8-bit. 100 = the channel's true maximum (nothing clipped). Lower it (e.g. 99.9) only if a hot pixel pins the max and squashes the signal.

params.label: Chunk size X
params.tip: Zarr chunk size in the X dimension. Larger chunks improve sequential read performance; smaller chunks improve random access.

params.label: Chunk size Y
params.tip: Zarr chunk size in the Y dimension.
